```typescript
import { MessageModule } from 'primeng/message';
import { ToastModule } from 'primeng/toast';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';
import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-toast />
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <form novalidate (submit)="onSubmit($event)" class="flex flex-col gap-4">
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <p-inputnumber inputId="sf_integeronly" [formField]="exampleForm.value" />
                    @if (exampleForm.value().touched() && exampleForm.value().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.value().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputNumberModule, MessageModule, ToastModule, ButtonModule, FormField]
})
export class InputNumberSignalFormsDemo {
    messageService = inject(MessageService);

    model = signal<{ value: number | null }>({ value: null });

    exampleForm = form(this.model, (path) => {
        required(path.value, { when: ({ state }) => state.touched(), message: 'Value is required.' });
    });

    onSubmit(event: Event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        submit(this.exampleForm, async () => {
            this.messageService.add({ severity: 'success', summary: 'Success', detail: 'Form is submitted', life: 3000 });
        });
    }
}
```

## Sizes

InputNumber provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputNumberModule } from 'primeng/inputnumber';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-4">
            <p-inputnumber [(ngModel)]="value1" size="small" placeholder="Small" mode="currency" currency="USD" locale="en-US" />
            <p-inputnumber [(ngModel)]="value2" placeholder="Normal" mode="currency" currency="USD" locale="en-US" />
            <p-inputnumber [(ngModel)]="value3" size="large" placeholder="Large" mode="currency" currency="USD" locale="en-US" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputNumberModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputNumberSizesDemo {
    value1!: number;

    value2!: number;

    value3!: number;
}
```

## Vertical

Buttons can also placed vertically by setting buttonLayout as vertical .

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputNumberModule } from 'primeng/inputnumber';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputnumber [(ngModel)]="value1" [showButtons]="true" buttonLayout="vertical" spinnerMode="vertical" inputId="vertical" [inputStyle]="{ width: '3rem' }">
                <ng-template #incrementbuttonicon>
                    <span class="pi pi-plus"></span>
                </ng-template>
                <ng-template #decrementbuttonicon>
                    <span class="pi pi-minus"></span>
                </ng-template>
            </p-inputnumber>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputNumberModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputNumberVerticalDemo {
    value1: number = 50;
}
```

## Input Number

InputNumber is an input component to provide numerical input.

### Props

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| dt | Object \| undefined | undefined | Defines scoped design tokens of the component. |
| unstyled | boolean \| undefined | undefined | Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles. |
| pt | PassThrough<I, InputNumberPassThroughOptions<I>> | undefined | Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component. |
| ptOptions | PassThroughOptions \| undefined | undefined | Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component. |
| required | boolean \| undefined | false | There must be a value (if set). |
| invalid | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have invalid state style. |
| disabled | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have disabled state style. |
| name | string \| undefined | undefined | When present, it specifies that the name of the input. |
| fluid | boolean \| undefined | false | Spans 100% width of the container when enabled. |
| variant | "filled" \| "outlined" \| undefined | 'outlined' | Specifies the input variant of the component. |
| size | "small" \| "large" \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the size of the component. |
| inputSize | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the visible width of the input element in characters. |
| pattern | string \| readonly RegExp[] \| null \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the value must match the pattern. |
| min | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The value must be greater than or equal to the value. |
| max | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The value must be less than or equal to the value. |
| step | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | Unless the step is set to the any literal, the value must be min + an integral multiple of the step. |
| minlength | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The number of characters (code points) must not be less than the value of the attribute, if non-empty. |
| maxlength | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The number of characters (code points) must not exceed the value of the attribute. |
| showButtons | boolean | - | Displays spinner buttons. |
| format | boolean | - | Whether to format the value. |
| buttonLayout | "stacked" \| "horizontal" \| "vertical" | - | Layout of the buttons, valid values are "stacked" (default), "horizontal" and "vertical". |
| inputId | string \| undefined | - | Identifier of the focus input to match a label defined for the component. |
| placeholder | string \| undefined | - | Advisory information to display on input. |
| tabindex | number \| undefined | - | Specifies tab order of the element. |
| title | string \| undefined | - | Title text of the input text. |
| ariaLabelledBy | string \| undefined | - | Specifies one or more IDs in the DOM that labels the input field. |
| ariaDescribedBy | string \| undefined | - | Specifies one or more IDs in the DOM that describes the input field. |
| ariaLabel | string \| undefined | - | Used to define a string that labels the input element. |
| ariaRequired | boolean \| undefined | - | Used to indicate that user input is required on an element before a form can be submitted. |
| autocomplete | string \| undefined | - | Used to define a string that autocomplete attribute the current element. |
| incrementButtonClass | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the increment button. |
| decrementButtonClass | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the decrement button. |
| incrementButtonIcon | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the increment button. |
| decrementButtonIcon | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the decrement button. |
| readonly | boolean \| undefined | - | When present, it specifies that an input field is read-only. |
| allowEmpty | boolean | - | Determines whether the input field is empty. |
| locale | string \| undefined | - | Locale to be used in formatting. |
| localeMatcher | InputNumberLocaleMatcher \| undefined | - | The locale matching algorithm to use. Possible values are "lookup" and "best fit"; the default is "best fit". See Locale Negotiation for details. |
| mode | "decimal" \| "currency" \| "percent" \| "unit" | - | Defines the behavior of the component, valid values are "decimal" and "currency". |
| currency | string \| undefined | - | The currency to use in currency formatting. Possible values are the ISO 4217 currency codes, such as "USD" for the US dollar, "EUR" for the euro, or "CNY" for the Chinese RMB. There is no default value; if the style is "currency", the currency property must be provided. |
| currencyDisplay | InputNumberCurrencyDisplay \| undefined | - | How to display the currency in currency formatting. Possible values are "symbol" to use a localized currency symbol such as €, ü"code" to use the ISO currency code, "name" to use a localized currency name such as "dollar"; the default is "symbol". |
| useGrouping | boolean | - | Whether to use grouping separators, such as thousands separators or thousand/lakh/crore separators. |
| minFractionDigits | number \| undefined | - | The minimum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are from 0 to 20; the default for plain number and percent formatting is 0; the default for currency formatting is the number of minor unit digits provided by the ISO 4217 currency code list (2 if the list doesn't provide that information). |
| maxFractionDigits | number \| undefined | - | The maximum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are from 0 to 20; the default for plain number formatting is the larger of minimumFractionDigits and 3; the default for currency formatting is the larger of minimumFractionDigits and the number of minor unit digits provided by the ISO 4217 currency code list (2 if the list doesn't provide that information). |
| prefix | string \| undefined | - | Text to display before the value. |
| suffix | string \| undefined | - | Text to display after the value. |
| inputStyle | { [klass: string]: any } \| null \| undefined | - | Inline style of the input field. |
| inputStyleClass | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the input field. |
| showClear | boolean | - | When enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value. |
| autofocus | boolean \| undefined | - | When present, it specifies that the component should automatically get focus on load. |

### Emits

| Name | Parameters | Description |
|------|------------|-------------|
| onInput | event: InputNumberInputEvent | Callback to invoke on input. |
| onFocus | event: Event | Callback to invoke when the component receives focus. |
| onBlur | event: Event | Callback to invoke when the component loses focus. |
| onKeyDown | event: KeyboardEvent | Callback to invoke on input key press. |
| onClear | value: void | Callback to invoke when clear token is clicked. |

### Templates

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| clearicon | TemplateRef<void> \| undefined | Custom clear icon template. |
| incrementbuttonicon | TemplateRef<void> \| undefined | Custom increment button icon template. |
| decrementbuttonicon | TemplateRef<void> \| undefined | Custom decrement button icon template. |

## Pass Through Options

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| host | PassThroughOption<HTMLElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the host's DOM element. |
| root | PassThroughOption<HTMLElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the root's DOM element. |
| pcInputText | InputTextPassThrough | Used to pass attributes to the InputText component. |
| clearIcon | PassThroughOption<HTMLSpanElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the clear icon's DOM element. |
| buttonGroup | PassThroughOption<HTMLSpanElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the button group's DOM element. |
| incrementButton | PassThroughOption<HTMLButtonElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the increment button's DOM element. |
| decrementButton | PassThroughOption<HTMLButtonElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the decrement button's DOM element. |
| incrementButtonIcon | PassThroughOption<HTMLSpanElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the increment button icon's DOM element. |
| decrementButtonIcon | PassThroughOption<HTMLSpanElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the decrement button icon's DOM element. |

## Theming

### CSS Classes

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| p-inputnumber | Class name of the root element |
| p-inputnumber-input | Class name of the input element |
| p-inputnumber-button-group | Class name of the button group element |
| p-inputnumber-increment-button | Class name of the increment button element |
| p-inputnumber-decrement-button | Class name of the decrement button element |
| p-autocomplete-clear-icon | Class name of the clear icon |

### Design Tokens

| Token | CSS Variable | Description |
|-------|--------------|-------------|
| inputnumber.transition.duration | --p-inputnumber-transition-duration | Transition duration of root |
| inputnumber.button.width | --p-inputnumber-button-width | Width of button |
| inputnumber.button.border.radius | --p-inputnumber-button-border-radius | Border radius of button |
| inputnumber.button.vertical.padding | --p-inputnumber-button-vertical-padding | Vertical padding of button |
| inputnumber.button.background | --p-inputnumber-button-background | Background of button |
| inputnumber.button.hover.background | --p-inputnumber-button-hover-background | Hover background of button |
| inputnumber.button.active.background | --p-inputnumber-button-active-background | Active background of button |
| inputnumber.button.border.color | --p-inputnumber-button-border-color | Border color of button |
| inputnumber.button.hover.border.color | --p-inputnumber-button-hover-border-color | Hover border color of button |
| inputnumber.button.active.border.color | --p-inputnumber-button-active-border-color | Active border color of button |
| inputnumber.button.color | --p-inputnumber-button-color | Color of button |
| inputnumber.button.hover.color | --p-inputnumber-button-hover-color | Hover color of button |
| inputnumber.button.active.color | --p-inputnumber-button-active-color | Active color of button |

---

# Angular Otp Input Component

Input Otp is used to enter one time passwords.

## Accessibility

Screen Reader Input OTP uses a set of InputText components, refer to the InputText component for more information about the screen reader support.

## Basic

A one-time password input split into individual digit fields.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpBasicDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## Controlled

InputOtp can be used as a controlled component with ngModel .

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-6">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" />
            <div class="flex items-center gap-4">
                <span class="text-sm text-muted-color">
                    Value: <strong class="text-color">{{ value || '' }}</strong>
                </span>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" size="small" (click)="value = ''">Reset</button>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, ButtonModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpControlledDemo {
    value: any = '';
}
```

## Disabled

When disabled is present, the component becomes non-interactive.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" [disabled]="true" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpDisabledDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## Filled

Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" variant="filled" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpFilledDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## Integer Only

When integerOnly is present, only integers can be accepted as input.

## Mask

Enable the mask option to hide the values in the input fields.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" [mask]="true" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpMaskDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## preview-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col gap-10 justify-between h-full max-w-xs mx-auto">
            <div>
                <div class="font-bold text-color text-lg">Check your email for OTP</div>
                <div class="text-surface-500 mt-1">Please enter the 6-digit code sent to your email address to reset your password.</div>
            </div>
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" [length]="6" [integerOnly]="true" class="w-full *:flex-1 *:aspect-square **:text-2xl! **:font-bold! my-auto gap-3" />
            <div>
                <div class="text-sm text-muted-color mb-2">
                    Didn't receive code?
                    <button pButton variant="text" class="text-sm">Resend</button>
                </div>
                <button pButton class="w-full">Change password</button>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, ButtonModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpPreviewDemo {
    value: any = '482';
}
```

## Sample

A sample UI implementation with templating and additional elements.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <div class="flex flex-col items-center">
                <div class="font-bold text-xl mb-2">Authenticate Your Account</div>
                <p class="text-sm text-muted-color block mb-8">Please enter the code sent to your phone.</p>
                <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value" [length]="6">
                    <ng-template #input let-token let-events="events" let-index="index">
                        <input type="text" [maxLength]="1" (input)="events.input($event)" (keydown)="events.keydown($event)" [attr.value]="token" class="custom-otp-input" />
                        @if (index === 3) {
                            <div class="px-4">
                                <i class="pi pi-minus"></i>
                            </div>
                        }
                    </ng-template>
                </p-inputotp>
                <div class="flex justify-between mt-8 self-stretch">
                    <button type="button" pButton [link]="true" class="p-0">Resend Code</button>
                    <button type="button" pButton>Submit Code</button>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, ButtonModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpSampleDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## signal-forms-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component, inject, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { form, FormField, required, submit, validate } from '@angular/forms/signals';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';
import { MessageModule } from 'primeng/message';
import { ToastModule } from 'primeng/toast';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';
import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-toast />
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <form novalidate (submit)="onSubmit($event)" class="flex flex-col gap-4">
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <p-inputotp [formField]="exampleForm.value" />
                    @if (exampleForm.value().touched() && exampleForm.value().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.value().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, MessageModule, ToastModule, ButtonModule, FormField]
})
export class InputOtpSignalFormsDemo {
    messageService = inject(MessageService);

    model = signal({ value: '' });

    exampleForm = form(this.model, (path) => {
        required(path.value, { when: ({ state }) => state.touched(), message: 'Passcode is required.' });
        validate(path.value, ({ value, state }) => {
            if (!state.touched() || !value() || value().length >= 4) return undefined;
            return { kind: 'minLength', message: 'Please enter all 4 digits.' };
        });
    });

    onSubmit(event: Event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        submit(this.exampleForm, async () => {
            this.messageService.add({ severity: 'success', summary: 'Success', detail: 'Form is submitted', life: 3000 });
        });
    }
}
```

## Sizes

InputOtp provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-4">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value1" size="small" />
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value2" />
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value3" size="large" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpSizesDemo {
    value1: any;

    value2: any;

    value3: any;
}
```

## Template

Define a template with your own UI elements with bindings to the provided events and attributes to replace the default design.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputOtpModule } from 'primeng/inputotp';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputotp [(ngModel)]="value">
                <ng-template #input let-token let-events="events">
                    <input class="custom-otp-input" (keydown)="events.keydown($event)" (input)="events.input($event)" type="text" [attr.value]="token" [maxLength]="1" />
                </ng-template>
            </p-inputotp>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputOtpModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputOtpTemplateDemo {
    value: any;
}
```

## Input Otp

Input Otp is used to enter one time passwords.

### Props

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| dt | Object \| undefined | undefined | Defines scoped design tokens of the component. |
| unstyled | boolean \| undefined | undefined | Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles. |
| pt | PassThrough<I, InputOtpPassThroughOptions<I>> | undefined | Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component. |
| ptOptions | PassThroughOptions \| undefined | undefined | Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component. |
| required | boolean \| undefined | false | There must be a value (if set). |
| invalid | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have invalid state style. |
| disabled | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have disabled state style. |
| name | string \| undefined | undefined | When present, it specifies that the name of the input. |
| readonly | boolean | - | When present, it specifies that an input field is read-only. |
| tabindex | number \| null | - | Index of the element in tabbing order. |
| length | number | - | Number of characters to initiate. |
| styleClass | string \| undefined | - | Style class of the input element. |
| mask | boolean | - | Mask pattern. |
| integerOnly | boolean | - | When present, it specifies that an input field is integer-only. |
| autofocus | boolean | - | When present, it specifies that the component should automatically get focus on load. |
| variant | InputVariant \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the input variant of the component. |
| size | InputSize \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the size of the component. |

### Emits

| Name | Parameters | Description |
|------|------------|-------------|
| onChange | event: InputOtpChangeEvent | Callback to invoke on value change. |
| onFocus | event: Event | Callback to invoke when the component receives focus. |
| onBlur | event: Event | Callback to invoke when the component loses focus. |

### Templates

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| input | TemplateRef<InputOtpInputTemplateContext> \| undefined | Custom input template. |

## Pass Through Options

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| host | PassThroughOption<HTMLElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the host's DOM element. |
| root | PassThroughOption<HTMLDivElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the root's DOM element. |
| pcInputText | InputTextPassThrough | Used to pass attributes to the InputText component. |

## Theming

### CSS Classes

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| p-inputotp | Class name of the root element |
| p-inputotp-input | Class name of the input element |

### Design Tokens

| Token | CSS Variable | Description |
|-------|--------------|-------------|
| inputotp.gap | --p-inputotp-gap | Gap of root |
| inputotp.input.width | --p-inputotp-input-width | Width of input |
| inputotp.input.sm.width | --p-inputotp-input-sm-width | Width of input in small screens |
| inputotp.input.lg.width | --p-inputotp-input-lg-width | Width of input in large screens |

---

# Angular InputPassword Component

InputPassword is an enhanced input for password entry with strength metering, mask toggling, and controlled or uncontrolled usage.

## Accessibility

Screen Reader InputPassword is applied to a native input element so it implicitly includes any passed prop. Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with id prop or using aria-labelledby , aria-label props.

## Basic

InputPassword is applied to an input element with the pInputPassword directive.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordBasicDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## Clear Icon

Use a custom clear action to reset the password input.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { IconFieldModule } from 'primeng/iconfield';
import { InputIconModule } from 'primeng/inputicon';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { Times } from '@primeicons/angular/times';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-iconfield>
                <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" class="w-56" />
                @if (value) {
                    <p-inputicon (click)="value = ''" style="cursor: pointer">
                        <svg data-p-icon="times"></svg>
                    </p-inputicon>
                }
            </p-iconfield>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [IconFieldModule, InputIconModule, InputPasswordModule, FormsModule, Times]
})
export class InputPasswordCleariconDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## Disabled

When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input pInputPassword disabled placeholder="Disabled" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule]
})
export class InputPasswordDisabledDemo {}
```

## Filled

Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input pInputPassword variant="filled" [(ngModel)]="value" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordFilledDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## Float Label

FloatLabel visually integrates a label with its form element. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

## Fluid

Fluid spans the full width of the container.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <input pInputPassword [fluid]="true" [(ngModel)]="value" />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordFluidDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## Ifta Label

IftaLabel is used to create infield top aligned labels. Visit IftaLabel documentation for more information.

## Invalid

The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
            <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value1" [invalid]="!value1" placeholder="Password" />
            <input pInputPassword variant="filled" [(ngModel)]="value2" [invalid]="!value2" placeholder="Password" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordInvalidDemo {
    value1: string = '';

    value2: string = '';
}
```

## Popover

Combine a visibility toggle, strength meter, and requirements checklist into a fully custom password creation with Popover component.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { IconFieldModule } from 'primeng/iconfield';
import { InputIconModule } from 'primeng/inputicon';
import { Popover, PopoverModule } from 'primeng/popover';
import { ProgressBarModule } from 'primeng/progressbar';
import { TagModule } from 'primeng/tag';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { Eye } from '@primeicons/angular/eye';
import { EyeSlash } from '@primeicons/angular/eye-slash';
import { Shield } from '@primeicons/angular/shield';
import { Check } from '@primeicons/angular/check';
import { Times } from '@primeicons/angular/times';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <div class="flex flex-col gap-2">
                <p-iconfield>
                    <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" [(mask)]="mask" placeholder="Create a password" (focus)="op.show($event)" (blur)="op.hide()" />
                    <p-inputicon (click)="mask = !mask" style="cursor: pointer">
                        @if (mask) {
                            <svg data-p-icon="eye"></svg>
                        } @else {
                            <svg data-p-icon="eye-slash"></svg>
                        }
                    </p-inputicon>
                </p-iconfield>
                <p-popover #op>
                    <div class="flex flex-col gap-3 w-72">
                        <div class="flex items-center justify-between">
                            <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
                                <svg data-p-icon="shield" style="width: 1.25rem; height: 1.25rem"></svg>
                                <span class="font-semibold text-sm">Password Strength</span>
                            </div>
                            <p-tag [severity]="severity" [value]="label || '—'" [style.visibility]="label ? 'visible' : 'hidden'" />
                        </div>
                        <p-progressbar [value]="score" [showValue]="false" [style]="{ height: '6px' }" [color]="color" />
                        <ul class="flex flex-col gap-2 list-none m-0 p-0">
                            @for (rule of rules; track rule.id) {
                                <li class="flex items-center gap-2 text-xs">
                                    @if (rule.test(value)) {
                                        <svg data-p-icon="check" class="text-green-500"></svg>
                                    } @else {
                                        <svg data-p-icon="times" class="text-red-400"></svg>
                                    }
                                    <span [class]="rule.test(value) ? 'text-surface-500' : 'text-surface-700'">{{ rule.label }}</span>
                                </li>
                            }
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                </p-popover>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [IconFieldModule, InputIconModule, PopoverModule, ProgressBarModule, TagModule, InputPasswordModule, FormsModule, Eye, EyeSlash, Shield, Check, Times]
})
export class InputPasswordPopoverDemo {
    value: string = '';

    mask: boolean = true;

    rules = [
        { id: 'length', label: 'At least 12 characters long', test: (v: string) => v.length >= 12, weight: 20 },
        { id: 'uppercase', label: 'Contains uppercase letter', test: (v: string) => /[A-Z]/.test(v), weight: 20 },
        { id: 'lowercase', label: 'Contains lowercase letter', test: (v: string) => /[a-z]/.test(v), weight: 20 },
        { id: 'number', label: 'Contains number', test: (v: string) => /[0-9]/.test(v), weight: 20 },
        { id: 'special', label: 'Contains special character (!@#$...)', test: (v: string) => /[^a-zA-Z0-9]/.test(v), weight: 20 }
    ];

    get score(): number {
        if (!this.value) return 0;
        return this.rules.reduce((acc, rule) => acc + (rule.test(this.value) ? rule.weight : 0), 0);
    }

    get severity(): 'danger' | 'warn' | 'info' | 'success' {
        if (this.score <= 20) return 'danger';
        if (this.score <= 40) return 'warn';
        if (this.score <= 60) return 'info';
        return 'success';
    }

    get color(): string {
        switch (this.severity) {
            case 'danger':
                return 'var(--p-red-500)';
            case 'warn':
                return 'var(--p-amber-500)';
            case 'info':
                return 'var(--p-blue-500)';
            case 'success':
                return 'var(--p-green-500)';
        }
    }

    get label(): string {
        if (this.score === 0) return '';
        if (this.score <= 20) return 'Too Weak';
        if (this.score <= 40) return 'Weak';
        if (this.score <= 60) return 'Fair';
        if (this.score <= 80) return 'Strong';
        return 'Very Strong';
    }
}
```

## preview-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { ChipModule } from 'primeng/chip';
import { IconFieldModule } from 'primeng/iconfield';
import { InputIconModule } from 'primeng/inputicon';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { Lock } from '@primeicons/angular/lock';
import { Eye } from '@primeicons/angular/eye';
import { EyeSlash } from '@primeicons/angular/eye-slash';
import { Check } from '@primeicons/angular/check';
import { Times } from '@primeicons/angular/times';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <div class="max-w-2xs w-full">
                <div class="text-sm font-medium text-color mb-2">Create a password</div>
                <p-iconfield>
                    <p-inputicon>
                        <svg data-p-icon="lock"></svg>
                    </p-inputicon>
                    <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" [(mask)]="mask" [fluid]="true" />
                    <p-inputicon (click)="mask = !mask" style="cursor: pointer">
                        @if (mask) {
                            <svg data-p-icon="eye" [size]="16"></svg>
                        } @else {
                            <svg data-p-icon="eye-slash" [size]="16"></svg>
                        }
                    </p-inputicon>
                </p-iconfield>
                <div class="mt-4 flex items-center flex-wrap gap-1.5">
                    @for (rule of rules; track rule.label) {
                        <p-chip [class]="'py-0.75! pl-1.5! pr-2.5! text-xs! ' + (rule.test(value) ? 'text-green-600! dark:text-green-400!' : 'text-surface-500! dark:text-surface-400!')">
                            <span
                                [class]="
                                    'size-4 -ml-0.75 inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full ' +
                                    (rule.test(value) ? 'bg-green-600 text-surface-0 dark:bg-green-400 dark:text-surface-900' : 'bg-surface-200 dark:bg-surface-700 text-surface-500 dark:text-surface-400')
                                "
                            >
                                @if (rule.test(value)) {
                                    <svg data-p-icon="check" [size]="12"></svg>
                                } @else {
                                    <svg data-p-icon="times" [size]="12"></svg>
                                }
                            </span>
                            {{ rule.label }}
                        </p-chip>
                    }
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [ChipModule, IconFieldModule, InputIconModule, InputPasswordModule, FormsModule, Lock, Eye, EyeSlash, Check, Times]
})
export class InputPasswordPreviewDemo {
    value: string = '1234';

    mask: boolean = true;

    rules = [
        { label: '8+ characters', test: (v: string) => v.length >= 8 },
        { label: 'Number', test: (v: string) => /\d/.test(v) },
        { label: 'Uppercase letter', test: (v: string) => /[A-Z]/.test(v) },
        { label: 'Special character', test: (v: string) => /[^a-zA-Z0-9]/.test(v) }
    ];
}
```

## Requirements

Password requirements checklist with real-time validation feedback.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { CheckCircle } from '@primeicons/angular/check-circle';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <div class="flex flex-col gap-2 w-64">
                <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" placeholder="Enter password" />
                <ul class="flex flex-col gap-2 list-none ms-1 my-1 p-0">
                    @for (req of requirements; track req.id) {
                        <li class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm transition-all duration-300">
                            <svg data-p-icon="check-circle" [class]="'transition-all duration-300 ease-out ' + (req.test(value) ? 'text-green-500 scale-110 opacity-100' : 'text-surface-400 scale-90 opacity-70')"></svg>
                            <span
                                [class]="
                                    'transition-all duration-300 ease-out ' + (req.test(value) ? 'text-green-700 dark:text-green-400 line-through decoration-2 decoration-green-500/70' : 'text-surface-400 dark:text-surface-300 dark:opacity-70')
                                "
                            >
                                {{ req.label }}
                            </span>
                        </li>
                    }
                </ul>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule, CheckCircle]
})
export class InputPasswordRequirementsDemo {
    value: string = '';

    requirements = [
        { id: 'minLength', label: 'At least 12 characters', test: (v: string) => v.length >= 12 },
        { id: 'uppercase', label: 'Contains uppercase letter', test: (v: string) => /[A-Z]/.test(v) },
        { id: 'lowercase', label: 'Contains lowercase letter', test: (v: string) => /[a-z]/.test(v) },
        { id: 'number', label: 'Contains number', test: (v: string) => /[0-9]/.test(v) },
        { id: 'symbol', label: 'Contains special character', test: (v: string) => /[^a-zA-Z0-9]/.test(v) }
    ];
}
```

## signal-forms-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component, inject, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { form, FormField, required, submit } from '@angular/forms/signals';
import { MessageModule } from 'primeng/message';
import { ToastModule } from 'primeng/toast';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-toast />
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <form novalidate (submit)="onSubmit($event)" class="flex flex-col gap-4">
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <input pInputPassword [formField]="exampleForm.value" autocomplete="off" placeholder="Password" />
                    @if (exampleForm.value().touched() && exampleForm.value().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.value().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [MessageModule, ToastModule, ButtonModule, InputPasswordModule, FormField]
})
export class InputPasswordSignalFormsDemo {
    messageService = inject(MessageService);

    model = signal({ value: '' });

    exampleForm = form(this.model, (path) => {
        required(path.value, { when: ({ state }) => state.touched(), message: 'Password is required.' });
    });

    onSubmit(event: Event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        submit(this.exampleForm, async () => {
            this.messageService.add({ severity: 'success', summary: 'Success', detail: 'Form is submitted', life: 3000 });
        });
    }
}
```

## Sizes

InputPassword provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-4">
            <input pInputPassword pSize="small" [(ngModel)]="value1" placeholder="Small" />
            <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value2" placeholder="Normal" />
            <input pInputPassword pSize="large" [(ngModel)]="value3" placeholder="Large" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordSizesDemo {
    value1: string = '';

    value2: string = '';

    value3: string = '';
}
```

## Strength Meter

Visualize the overall password strength with an animated progress bar and a severity-based label that adapts as the password improves.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { ProgressBarModule } from 'primeng/progressbar';
import { TagModule } from 'primeng/tag';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <div class="flex flex-col gap-2 w-64">
                <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" placeholder="Enter password" />
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-2" [style.visibility]="strengthInfo ? 'visible' : 'hidden'">
                    <p-progressbar [value]="strengthInfo?.percent ?? 0" [showValue]="false" [style]="{ height: '6px' }" [color]="strengthInfo?.color" />
                    <div class="flex justify-end">
                        <p-tag [severity]="strengthInfo?.severity ?? 'info'" [value]="strengthInfo?.label ?? '—'" />
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [ProgressBarModule, TagModule, InputPasswordModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputPasswordStrengthDemo {
    value: string = '';

    get strengthInfo() {
        const level = this.getStrength(this.value);
        return level ? this.strengthMap[level] : null;
    }

    getStrength(value: string): string | null {
        if (!value) return null;
        
        let score = 0;
        if (value.length >= 8) score++;
        if (value.length >= 12) score++;
        if (/[A-Z]/.test(value) && /[a-z]/.test(value)) score++;
        if (/[0-9]/.test(value)) score++;
        if (/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/.test(value)) score++;
        
        if (score <= 1) return 'weak';
        if (score <= 2) return 'medium';
        if (score <= 3) return 'strong';
        return 'very-strong';
    }
}
```

## Toggle Mask

Adding a toggle icon to show or hide the password, allowing users to verify their input.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { IconFieldModule } from 'primeng/iconfield';
import { InputIconModule } from 'primeng/inputicon';
import { InputPasswordModule } from 'primeng/inputpassword';
import { Eye } from '@primeicons/angular/eye';
import { EyeSlash } from '@primeicons/angular/eye-slash';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-iconfield>
                <input pInputPassword [(ngModel)]="value" [(mask)]="mask" />
                <p-inputicon (click)="mask = !mask" style="cursor: pointer">
                    @if (mask) {
                        <svg data-p-icon="eye"></svg>
                    } @else {
                        <svg data-p-icon="eye-slash"></svg>
                    }
                </p-inputicon>
            </p-iconfield>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [IconFieldModule, InputIconModule, InputPasswordModule, FormsModule, Eye, EyeSlash]
})
export class InputPasswordTogglemaskDemo {
    value: string = '';

    mask: boolean = true;
}
```

## Input Password

InputPassword directive is an extension to standard input element with mask toggling.

### Props

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| dt | Object \| undefined | undefined | Defines scoped design tokens of the component. |
| unstyled | boolean \| undefined | undefined | Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles. |
| pt | PassThrough<I, InputPasswordPassThroughOptions<I>> | undefined | Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component. |
| ptOptions | PassThroughOptions \| undefined | undefined | Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component. |
| mask | boolean | true | Whether the password is masked. |

## Pass Through Options

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| root | PassThroughOption<HTMLInputElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the root's DOM element. |

## Theming

### CSS Classes

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| p-password | The class of root element |

---

# Angular InputTags Component

InputTags is used to enter multiple tags.

## Accessibility

Screen Reader The input tags component uses a listbox role with aria-orientation set to horizontal. Each tag has the option role with aria-label , aria-selected , aria-setsize and aria-posinset attributes. Keyboard Support Key Function Enter Adds the typed value as a tag. Backspace Removes the last tag when the input is empty. Left Arrow Moves focus to the previous tag. Right Arrow Moves focus to the next tag.

## Allow Duplicate

By default, duplicate values are not allowed. Set allowDuplicate to enable adding the same value multiple times.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" [allowDuplicate]="true" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsAllowDuplicateDemo {
    value: string[] = ['a', 'A', 'a'];
}
```

## Basic

InputTags is used to enter multiple tags. Press Enter to add a tag and Backspace to remove the last one.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsBasicDemo {
    value: string[] = ['Angular'];
}
```

## Delimiter

A custom delimiter like a comma can be used in addition to the Enter key to add tags. Enable addOnPaste to split pasted text using the delimiter.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" delimiter="," [addOnPaste]="true" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsDelimiterDemo {
    value: string[] = [];
}
```

## Disabled

When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" [disabled]="true" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsDisabledDemo {
    value: string[] = ['Angular'];
}
```

## Events

Use onAdd and onRemove callbacks to listen for tag changes.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" (onAdd)="onAddItem($event)" (onRemove)="onRemoveItem($event)" placeholder="Add tags and watch the event log..." fluid />
        <div class="mt-4 text-sm">
            @for (log of logs; track $index) {
                <p>{{ log }}</p>
            }
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsEventsDemo {
    value: string[] = ['Angular'];

    logs: string[] = [];

    onAddItem(event: any) {
        this.logs = [`Added: "${event.value}"`, ...this.logs.slice(0, 4)];
    }

    onRemoveItem(event: any) {
        this.logs = [`Removed: "${event.value}" at index ${event.index}`, ...this.logs.slice(0, 4)];
    }
}
```

## Filled

Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" variant="filled" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsFilledDemo {
    value: string[] = ['Angular'];
}
```

## Float Label

A floating label appears on top of the input field when focused.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { FloatLabelModule } from 'primeng/floatlabel';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-floatlabel>
            <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" inputId="tags" fluid />
            <label for="tags">Technologies</label>
        </p-floatlabel>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [FloatLabelModule, InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsFloatLabelDemo {
    value: string[] = [];
}
```

## Ifta Label

IftaLabel is used to create infield top aligned labels. Visit IftaLabel documentation for more information.

## Invalid

The invalid property is used to indicate an invalid state.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" [invalid]="value.length === 0" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsInvalidDemo {
    value: string[] = [];
}
```

## Max

The maximum number of tags is limited using the max property.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" [max]="5" fluid />
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsMaxDemo {
    value: string[] = ['Angular', 'React'];
}
```

## preview-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="max-w-3xs mx-auto flex flex-col">
            <label class="mb-2 text-sm font-medium">Frameworks</label>
            <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsPreviewDemo {
    value: string[] = ['React', 'JavaScript', 'Vue', 'Angular'];
}
```

## signal-forms-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component, inject, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { form, FormField, submit, validate } from '@angular/forms/signals';
import { MessageModule } from 'primeng/message';
import { ToastModule } from 'primeng/toast';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';
import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-toast />
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <form novalidate (submit)="onSubmit($event)" class="flex flex-col gap-4 w-full sm:w-56">
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <p-inputtags [formField]="exampleForm.tags" />
                    @if (exampleForm.tags().touched() && exampleForm.tags().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.tags().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [MessageModule, ToastModule, InputTagsModule, ButtonModule, FormField]
})
export class InputTagsSignalFormsDemo {
    messageService = inject(MessageService);

    model = signal<{ tags: string[] }>({ tags: [] });

    exampleForm = form(this.model, (path) => {
        validate(path.tags, ({ value, state }) => {
            if (!state.touched()) return null;
            return value().length > 0 ? null : { kind: 'minLength', message: 'At least one tag is required.' };
        });
    });

    onSubmit(event: Event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        submit(this.exampleForm, async () => {
            this.messageService.add({ severity: 'success', summary: 'Success', detail: 'Form is submitted', life: 3000 });
        });
    }
}
```

## Template

Custom content can be displayed for each tag using the item template.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { TagModule } from 'primeng/tag';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';
import { Times } from '@primeicons/angular/times';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-inputtags [(ngModel)]="value" fluid>
            <ng-template #item let-item let-i="index" let-onRemove="onRemove">
                <p-tag [rounded]="true">
                    {{ item }}
                    <svg data-p-icon="times" class="cursor-pointer ml-2" style="width: 12px; height: 12px" (click)="onRemove($event, i)"></svg>
                </p-tag>
            </ng-template>
        </p-inputtags>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [TagModule, InputTagsModule, FormsModule, Times]
})
export class InputTagsTemplateDemo {
    value: string[] = ['JavaScript', 'TypeScript'];
}
```

## Typeahead

When typeahead is enabled, a dropdown with suggestions is displayed as the user types. Use the completeMethod event to provide search results.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTagsModule } from 'primeng/inputtags';
import { LabelModule } from 'primeng/label';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-inputtags
                [(ngModel)]="values"
                [typeahead]="true"
                [group]="true"
                [allowDuplicate]="true"
                [suggestions]="filteredItems"
                (completeMethod)="search($event)"
                optionLabel="label"
                optionGroupLabel="label"
                optionGroupChildren="items"
                placeholder="Search technologies..."
                class="w-full md:w-56"
            />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTagsModule, LabelModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTagsTypeaheadDemo {
    values: any[] = [];

    items = [
        {
            label: 'Frontend',
            items: [{ label: 'React' }, { label: 'Vue' }, { label: 'Angular' }, { label: 'Svelte' }, { label: 'Next.js' }, { label: 'Nuxt' }]
        },
        {
            label: 'Backend',
            items: [{ label: 'Node.js' }, { label: 'Python' }, { label: 'Java' }, { label: 'Go' }, { label: 'Rust' }, { label: 'Ruby' }]
        }
    ];

    filteredItems: any[] = [];

    search(event: InputTagsCompleteEvent) {
        const query = event.query.toLowerCase();
        this.filteredItems = this.items
            .map((group) => ({
                ...group,
                items: group.items.filter((item) => item.label.toLowerCase().includes(query))
            }))
            .filter((group) => group.items.length > 0);
    }
}
```

## Input Tags

InputTags is used to enter multiple tags with optional typeahead suggestions.

### Props

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| dt | Object \| undefined | undefined | Defines scoped design tokens of the component. |
| unstyled | boolean \| undefined | undefined | Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles. |
| pt | PassThrough<I, InputTagsPassThroughOptions<I>> | undefined | Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component. |
| ptOptions | PassThroughOptions \| undefined | undefined | Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component. |
| required | boolean \| undefined | false | There must be a value (if set). |
| invalid | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have invalid state style. |
| disabled | boolean \| undefined | false | When present, it specifies that the component should have disabled state style. |
| name | string \| undefined | undefined | When present, it specifies that the name of the input. |
| fluid | boolean \| undefined | false | Spans 100% width of the container when enabled. |
| variant | "filled" \| "outlined" \| undefined | 'outlined' | Specifies the input variant of the component. |
| size | "small" \| "large" \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the size of the component. |
| inputSize | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the visible width of the input element in characters. |
| pattern | string \| readonly RegExp[] \| null \| undefined | undefined | Specifies the value must match the pattern. |
| min | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The value must be greater than or equal to the value. |
| step | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | Unless the step is set to the any literal, the value must be min + an integral multiple of the step. |
| minlength | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The number of characters (code points) must not be less than the value of the attribute, if non-empty. |
| maxlength | number \| null \| undefined | undefined | The number of characters (code points) must not exceed the value of the attribute. |
| placeholder | string \| undefined | - | Advisory information to display on input. |
| max | number \| undefined | - | Maximum number of tags allowed. |
| delimiter | string \| RegExp \| undefined | - | Delimiter character or regex to split input into tags. |
| allowDuplicate | boolean | false | Whether to allow duplicate tags. |
| addOnBlur | boolean | false | Whether to add a tag when the input loses focus. |
| addOnTab | boolean | false | Whether to add a tag on tab key press. |
| addOnPaste | boolean | false | Whether to add tags on paste event. |
| tabindex | number \| undefined | - | Index of the element in tabbing order. |
| typeahead | boolean | false | Whether typeahead mode is active. |
| suggestions | any[] | - | An array of suggestions to display in the dropdown. |
| optionLabel | string \| ((item: any) => string) \| undefined | - | Property name or getter function for option label. |
| optionValue | string \| ((item: any) => any) \| undefined | - | Property name or getter function for option value. |
| optionDisabled | string \| ((item: any) => boolean) \| undefined | - | Property name or getter function for disabled state. |
| optionGroupLabel | string | 'label' | Field name for option group label. |
| optionGroupChildren | string | 'items' | Field name for option group children. |
| group | boolean | false | Whether to display options as grouped. |
| delay | number | 300 | Delay in ms between keystrokes before triggering search. |
| minQueryLength | number | 1 | Minimum number of characters to initiate a search. |
| completeOnFocus | boolean | false | Whether to trigger search when input receives focus. |
| scrollHeight | string | '200px' | Maximum height of the suggestions panel. |
| appendTo | HTMLElement \| ElementRef \| TemplateRef<any> \| "self" \| "body" \| null \| undefined | 'body' | Target element to attach the overlay. |
| overlayOptions | OverlayOptions \| undefined | - | Configuration for the overlay element. |
| motionOptions | MotionOptions \| undefined | - | Animation options for the overlay. |
| panelStyle | { [klass: string]: any } \| undefined | - | Inline style of the overlay panel. |
| panelStyleClass | string \| undefined | - | CSS class of the overlay panel. |
| inputStyle | { [klass: string]: any } \| undefined | - | Inline style of the input field. |
| inputStyleClass | string \| undefined | - | CSS class of the input field. |
| autoOptionFocus | boolean | false | Whether to auto-focus first option when overlay shows. |
| focusOnHover | boolean | true | Whether hover focuses an option. |

### Emits

| Name | Parameters | Description |
|------|------------|-------------|
| completeMethod | event: InputTagsCompleteEvent | Callback to invoke to search for suggestions. |
| onSelect | event: InputTagsSelectEvent | Callback to invoke when a suggestion is selected. |
| onShow | value: void | Callback to invoke when the overlay is shown. |
| onHide | value: void | Callback to invoke when the overlay is hidden. |

## Pass Through Options

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| host | PassThroughOption<HTMLElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the host's DOM element. |
| root | PassThroughOption<HTMLDivElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the root's DOM element. |
| pcChip | PassThroughOption<ChipPassThrough, I> | Used to pass attributes to the Chip component. |
| chipIcon | PassThroughOption<HTMLSpanElement, I> | Used to pass attributes to the chip icon's DOM element. |
| pcAutoComplete | PassThroughOption<AutoCompletePassThrough, I> | Used to pass attributes to the AutoComplete component. |

## Theming

### CSS Classes

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| p-inputtags |  |
| p-inputtags-item |  |
| p-inputtags-input |  |

### Design Tokens

| Token | CSS Variable | Description |
|-------|--------------|-------------|
| inputtags.background | --p-inputtags-background | Background of root |
| inputtags.disabled.background | --p-inputtags-disabled-background | Disabled background of root |
| inputtags.filled.background | --p-inputtags-filled-background | Filled background of root |
| inputtags.filled.hover.background | --p-inputtags-filled-hover-background | Filled hover background of root |
| inputtags.filled.focus.background | --p-inputtags-filled-focus-background | Filled focus background of root |
| inputtags.border.color | --p-inputtags-border-color | Border color of root |
| inputtags.hover.border.color | --p-inputtags-hover-border-color | Hover border color of root |
| inputtags.focus.border.color | --p-inputtags-focus-border-color | Focus border color of root |
| inputtags.invalid.border.color | --p-inputtags-invalid-border-color | Invalid border color of root |
| inputtags.color | --p-inputtags-color | Color of root |
| inputtags.disabled.color | --p-inputtags-disabled-color | Disabled color of root |
| inputtags.shadow | --p-inputtags-shadow | Shadow of root |
| inputtags.padding.x | --p-inputtags-padding-x | Padding x of root |
| inputtags.padding.y | --p-inputtags-padding-y | Padding y of root |
| inputtags.border.radius | --p-inputtags-border-radius | Border radius of root |
| inputtags.focus.ring.width | --p-inputtags-focus-ring-width | Focus ring width of root |
| inputtags.focus.ring.style | --p-inputtags-focus-ring-style | Focus ring style of root |
| inputtags.focus.ring.color | --p-inputtags-focus-ring-color | Focus ring color of root |
| inputtags.focus.ring.offset | --p-inputtags-focus-ring-offset | Focus ring offset of root |
| inputtags.focus.ring.shadow | --p-inputtags-focus-ring-shadow | Focus ring shadow of root |
| inputtags.transition.duration | --p-inputtags-transition-duration | Transition duration of root |
| inputtags.gap | --p-inputtags-gap | Gap of root |
| inputtags.item.border.radius | --p-inputtags-item-border-radius | Border radius of item |
| inputtags.item.focus.background | --p-inputtags-item-focus-background | Focus background of item |

---

# Angular InputText Component

InputText is an extension to standard input element with icons and theming.

## Accessibility

Screen Reader InputText component renders a native input element that implicitly includes any passed prop. Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with id prop or using aria-labelledby , aria-label props.

## Basic

InputText is used as a controlled input with ngModel property.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input type="text" pInputText [(ngModel)]="value" placeholder="Enter text" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextBasicDemo {
    value: string;
}
```

## Clear Icon

Combine InputText with IconField to display a clear icon that resets the value when clicked.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { IconFieldModule } from 'primeng/iconfield';
import { InputIconModule } from 'primeng/inputicon';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';
import { Times } from '@primeicons/angular/times';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <p-iconfield>
                <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value" class="w-56" placeholder="Type something" />
                @if (value) {
                    <p-inputicon (click)="value = ''" style="cursor: pointer">
                        <svg data-p-icon="times"></svg>
                    </p-inputicon>
                }
            </p-iconfield>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [IconFieldModule, InputIconModule, InputTextModule, FormsModule, Times]
})
export class InputTextCleariconDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## Disabled

When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input pInputText disabled placeholder="Disabled" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule]
})
export class InputTextDisabledDemo {}
```

## Filled

Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input type="text" pInputText [(ngModel)]="value" variant="filled" placeholder="Enter text" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextFilledDemo {
    value: string;
}
```

## Float Label

FloatLabel visually integrates a label with its form element. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

## Fluid

The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <input type="text" pInputText [(ngModel)]="value" fluid />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextFluidDemo {
    value: string;
}
```

## Help Text

An advisory text can be defined with the semantic small tag.

## icons-doc

Icons can be placed inside an input element by wrapping both the input and the icon with an element that has either .p-input-icon-left or p-input-icon-right class.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
            <span class="p-input-icon-left">
                <i class="pi pi-search"></i>
                <input type="text" pInputText [(ngModel)]="value" />
            </span>
            <span class="p-input-icon-right">
                <i class="pi pi-spin pi-spinner"></i>
                <input type="text" pInputText [(ngModel)]="value2" />
            </span>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextIconsDemo {
    value: string | undefined;

    value2: string | undefined;
}
```

## Ifta Label

IftaLabel is used to create infield top aligned labels. Visit IftaLabel documentation for more information.

## Invalid

The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
            <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value1" [invalid]="!value1" placeholder="Enter text" />
            <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value2" [invalid]="!value2" variant="filled" placeholder="Enter text" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextInvalidDemo {
    value1: string | undefined;

    value2: string | undefined;
}
```

## keyfilter-doc

InputText has built-in key filtering support to block certain keys, refer to keyfilter page for more information.

## preview-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';
import { LabelModule } from 'primeng/label';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col gap-1.5 mx-auto w-full max-w-xs">
            <label pLabel for="basic-inputtext">Project Name</label>
            <input pInputText id="basic-inputtext" [(ngModel)]="value" placeholder="e.g. My Project" />
            <small class="text-surface-500">Used as the display name across your workspace</small>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, LabelModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextPreviewDemo {
    value: string = '';
}
```

## signal-forms-doc

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component, inject, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { email, form, FormField, required, submit } from '@angular/forms/signals';
import { MessageModule } from 'primeng/message';
import { ToastModule } from 'primeng/toast';
import { ButtonModule } from 'primeng/button';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';
import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

@Component({
    template: `
        <p-toast />
        <div class="flex justify-center">
            <form novalidate (submit)="onSubmit($event)" class="flex flex-col gap-4 w-full sm:w-56">
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <input pInputText type="text" id="sf_username" placeholder="Username" [formField]="exampleForm.username" />
                    @if (exampleForm.username().touched() && exampleForm.username().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.username().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <div class="flex flex-col gap-1">
                    <input pInputText type="email" id="sf_email" placeholder="Email" [formField]="exampleForm.email" />
                    @if (exampleForm.email().touched() && exampleForm.email().invalid()) {
                        @for (error of exampleForm.email().errors(); track error.kind) {
                            <p-message severity="error" size="small" variant="simple">{{ error.message }}</p-message>
                        }
                    }
                </div>
                <button pButton severity="secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
            </form>
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [MessageModule, ToastModule, ButtonModule, InputTextModule, FormField]
})
export class InputTextSignalFormsDemo {
    messageService = inject(MessageService);

    model = signal({ username: '', email: '' });

    exampleForm = form(this.model, (path) => {
        required(path.username, { when: ({ state }) => state.touched(), message: 'Username is required.' });
        required(path.email, { when: ({ state }) => state.touched(), message: 'Email is required.' });
        email(path.email, { message: 'Enter a valid email address.' });
    });

    onSubmit(event: Event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        submit(this.exampleForm, async () => {
            this.messageService.add({ severity: 'success', summary: 'Success', detail: 'Form is submitted', life: 3000 });
        });
    }
}
```

## Sizes

InputText provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the standard.

**Example:**

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms';
import { InputTextModule } from 'primeng/inputtext';

@Component({
    template: `
        <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-4">
            <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value1" type="text" pSize="small" placeholder="Small" />
            <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value2" type="text" placeholder="Normal" />
            <input pInputText [(ngModel)]="value3" type="text" pSize="large" placeholder="Large" />
        </div>
    `,
    standalone: true,
    imports: [InputTextModule, FormsModule]
})
export class InputTextSizesDemo {
    value1: string | undefined;

    value2: string | undefined;

    value3: string | undefined;
}
```

## Input Text

InputText directive is an extension to standard input element with theming.

### Props

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| dt | Object \| undefined | undefined | Defines scoped design tokens of the component. |
| unstyled | boolean \| undefined | undefined | Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles. |
| pt | PassThrough<I, InputTextPassThroughOptions<I>> | undefined | Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component. |
| ptOptions | PassThroughOptions \| undefined | undefined | Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component. |
